title: lithocarpus glutinosus
intro: Lithocarpus glutinosus, also known as the tanbark oak or Bur Oak, is a deciduous tree native to China and Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Lithocarpus glutinosus include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of the plant include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Lithocarpus glutinosus can be used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
